title: Monitoring
subtitle: Monitor DocFlow service health, API traffic, task queues, storage, and AI operations.

services.postgresHint: Database connectivity and query latency are healthy
services.redisHint: Cache and task queue connectivity are healthy
services.storageHint: Default storage and disk capacity are healthy

overview.staleHint: The scheduled collector may be offline. Check the Monitor and Worker services.
overview.servicesHint: Connectivity, latency, and capacity of platform dependencies

charts.requestsHint: Five-minute request volume over the last 24 hours
charts.errorsHint: Server errors as a percentage of all requests

alerts.title: Alert events
alerts.subtitle: Review, acknowledge, and track platform incidents and recoveries.
alerts.emptyHint: The platform is healthy. Triggered and recovered events will appear here.

rules.title: Alert rules
rules.subtitle: Configure thresholds, consecutive checks, severity, and notification cooldowns.
rules.formHint: Alerts trigger only after consecutive threshold breaches and resolve automatically.

channels.title: Notification channels
channels.subtitle: Send alert and recovery notifications by Webhook or email.
channels.formHint: Sensitive destinations are encrypted and only a masked value is returned.
channels.emptyHint: Add a Webhook for Teams, Slack, DingTalk, Feishu, or a custom incident system.
channels.targetRetained: Leave blank to retain the current destination

targetHint.webhook: Enter the complete HTTPS Webhook URL
targetHint.email: Email requires DOCFLOW_SMTP_* environment variables on the server
